---
title: Iterators and closures
description: Build lazy iterator pipelines and consume them safely.
---

## Iterators and closures

**Focus:** one `top_scores` entry that filters, transforms, limits, collects, and reports a score list.

[Open in Playground](https://playground.xelis.io/?%)

```rust filename="iterators.slx" copy
struct TopScoresRpcEvent {
    selected: u64[],
    total: u64
}

entry top_scores(scores: u64[], min_score: u64, limit: u32) -> u64[] {
    let selected: u64[] = scores
        .iter()
        .filter(|score: u64| { return score >= min_score })
        .map(|score: u64| { return score * 10 })
        .take(limit)
        .collect()

    let total: u64 = selected.iter().sum()
    fire_rpc_event(10, TopScoresRpcEvent { selected, total })
    return selected
}
```

## How it works

- `scores.iter()` creates the lazy iterator pipeline.
- `filter` keeps only values greater than or equal to `min_score`.
- `map` applies a multiplier to each selected score.
- `take(limit)` bounds the amount of work before `collect()` materializes the array.
- `selected.iter().sum()` consumes a second iterator to compute the total emitted in `TopScoresRpcEvent`.
